reasoning: To achieve 17 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200, Taylor Swift's 'The Life of a Showgirl' would have to maintain the top spot from its October 2025 debut across the entire fourth quarter (October through January). While its debut was a record-breaking 4.002 million units, second-week units dropped roughly 91% to 338,000 (a steeper percentage drop than 'The Tortured Poets Department'). By weeks 5-8, its numbers will naturally decline well under 100,000. Surviving 17 total consecutive weeks means remaining at #1 into February 2026, which requires weathering all Q4 major blockbuster drops and the enormous surge of Christmas/holiday catalog albums in December. No album in recent history has managed 17 consecutive weeks in the modern streaming era (Morgan Wallen's 'One Thing at a Time' achieved 15 consecutive weeks; TTPD did 12 consecutive weeks before a block). Given the historical precedent, the steep second-week drop, and the Christmas gauntlet, it is highly improbable this reaches 17 consecutive weeks. Setting at the lower bound allowed.
